author: Robert Carr
description: Open-faced ciabatta toast with whole-milk mozzarella, loose folds of prosciutto, pickle-brined arugula, onion, chopped pickles, and light Dijon mayo.

groceryIngredients.item: ciabatta roll or sturdy hoagie roll

ingredients: 1 small ciabatta roll or 2 slices ciabatta bread, split
1 ounce prosciutto, torn into bite-size pieces
Small handful baby arugula, patted dry
A few very thin onion slices
2 to 3 dill pickle slices, finely chopped
1 teaspoon pickle brine from the jar
instructions: Stir the mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, and 1/2 teaspoon pickle brine together in a small bowl.
Toast the ciabatta in a toaster or cut-side down in a dry skillet over medium heat for 2 to 4 minutes, until crisp at the edges.
Spread the Dijon mayo lightly over the warm toast.
Add the mozzarella slices and let them sit on the warm toast for 30 seconds so they soften slightly.
Drape the prosciutto over the mozzarella in loose folds instead of flat layers.
Toss the arugula, onion slices, chopped pickles, remaining 1/2 teaspoon pickle brine, and extra-virgin olive oil together with your fingers.
Pile the dressed arugula mixture over the prosciutto.
Eat open-faced, or close it like a sandwich if the bread pieces match.
notes: Prosciutto is thin, salty, and delicate, so fold it onto warm toast instead of cooking it hard.
Prosciutto and pickles are salty, so start with a light hand and add more only if you want it.
The mozzarella is mild; the Dijon mayo, arugula, onion, chopped pickles, olive oil, and pickle brine keep it from tasting flat.
Loose folds of prosciutto are easier to bite through than one flat sheet.
If the ciabatta is very chewy, toast it a little longer so it becomes easier to bite.
Serve right after assembly while the bread is crisp.
title: Prosciutto Mozzarella Arugula Toast
